name: Liver
description: Liver (from chicken, beef, or pork) is safe for cats in small, occasional amounts and is actually a nutrient-dense food cats naturally consume as obligate carnivores. However, it becomes dangerous when fed in excess due to its extremely high vitamin A content. Overconsumption causes vitamin A toxicity (hypervitaminosis A), a serious condition that damages bones and the nervous system.
safetyNotes: Safe amount: up to 5% of total diet occasionally; more than this risks vitamin A toxicity over time
Excess risk: hypervitaminosis A — causes painful bone deformations (especially neck and spine), joint stiffness, neurological issues
Raw liver risk: may carry Salmonella, Listeria, or other pathogens; lightly cooking reduces bacterial risk
Avoid seasoned or marinated liver — garlic, onion, and salt used in cooking are toxic to cats
Liver as an occasional treat is fine; never make it a daily food source

symptoms.name: While liver is not inherently toxic in small amounts
Abnormal Bone Development and Joint Pain
symptoms.onset: Progressive (develops over several weeks to months of a liver-heavy diet)
symptoms.notes: The excess Vitamin A triggers abnormal bony growths, particularly along the spine and joints, making it incredibly painful for the cat to move, groom, or even assume the posture to use the litter box

symptoms.name: Dermatological Abnormalities
symptoms.notes: Extreme skin sensitivity and peeling skin are hallmark superficial signs of Vitamin A building up to toxic levels in the cat's tissues

symptoms.name: Gastrointestinal Upset
symptoms.notes: Constipation often occurs secondary to the bone deformations; the cat experiences so much pain when trying to posture to defecate that they hold their stool in1. Severe weight loss also occurs as the cat becomes too weak or pained to eat normally

symptoms.name: Behavioral and Systemic Changes
symptoms.notes: As the toxicity affects the entire body and causes constant physical pain, the cat will become highly irritable, weak, and reluctant to be touched

symptoms.name: Fetal Deformities (in pregnant cats)
symptoms.notes: If a pregnant cat is fed a liver-heavy diet and consumes too much Vitamin A, it will cross the placenta and have severe, harmful developmental effects on the unborn kittens
